code:
def median(nums):
    if not nums: return None
    s = sorted(nums)
    n = len(s)
    mid = n // 2
    if n % 2:
        return s[mid]
    return (s[mid-1] + s[mid]) / 2
question: Why average two middle values for even-length input?
options: Median of even-length data is the mean of the two central order statistics
explain: The mathematical median of an even-length sample is the mean of the two central order statistics, not a single index. Returning s[n//2] biases the result upward by one position and violates the median symmetry property.

question: Why resolve + prefix check?
options: Prevents path traversal via .. escape; canonicalize then verify within root
explain: Concatenating untrusted input into a filesystem path lets an attacker submit dot-dot sequences and escape the intended directory. Canonicalize via path.resolve then verify the result starts with the allowed root (CWE-22).

question: Why buffer the channel to len(urls)?
options: Prevents goroutine leak when caller returns early without draining
explain: On early return goroutines block forever on an unbuffered channel with no receiver, leaking until process exit. Buffering to len(urls) lets them complete without a receiver waiting.

question: Why Arc<Mutex<u64>> rather than Arc<u64>?
options: Arc gives shared but immutable access; Mutex adds interior mutability
explain: Arc gives shared ownership but only immutable access to its contents, enforcing aliasing-XOR-mutability across threads. To mutate shared state safely, wrap in an atomic type or a Mutex; bare Arc<u64> is read-only.

code:
SELECT id, name, email
FROM users
WHERE status = 'active'
  AND created_at > '2025-01-01'
ORDER BY name;
question: Why ORDER BY name (column reference) over ORDER BY 2 (ordinal)?
options: Ordinal breaks silently if the SELECT list is reordered; column name is refactor-safe
explain: Positional ORDER BY couples sort order to column position, so any later reordering of the SELECT list silently changes results. Always sort by explicit column name or alias to make intent locally readable.
